question: What kind of loop is created when you type 'while True:' in Python?

options.A: A loop that runs exactly one time
options.B: An infinite loop that keeps repeating forever
options.C: A loop that only runs if a condition is False
options.D: A loop that deletes your code after running

question: Why do programmers use 'while True:' loops in robotics or video games?

options.A: To make the robot turn off immediately
options.B: To create a 'Main Loop' so the program constantly checks sensors and stays active
options.C: To automatically fix typing mistakes in the code
options.D: To speed up how fast numbers count

question: What Python keyword can you use inside a loop to instantly stop it and break out of it?
What does the command 'time.sleep(1)' do inside a fast-running loop?

options.B: It clears all variables and resets them to zero
options.C: It pauses the execution of the program for 1 second
options.D: It prints the current time on the screen

question:
Look at this code snippet:

while True:
    print("Hello")
    break

How many times will 'Hello' print on the screen?
